name: Comeketo Slack — per-person voice specimens (verbatim archive)
purpose: Raw verbatim quote archive captured from Slack. Each specimen is a real message Rodbot can study for cadence, punctuation, emoji habit, lexicon, and register. This is the source-of-truth when the handling.tone / handling.voice_adjustments fields on a people record refer to 'the voice from the Slack specimens'.
collection_method: First-pass Slack survey 2026-04-22 / 2026-04-23. Channels sampled: #a_catering_group (C062579HJH5, ~200 messages), #special-requests (C07H2P27U7N, ~200 messages), #general (C0279GNRPMZ, ~100 messages), #ai-topics (C0APZK6RFNC, all ~40 messages). Not exhaustive — a full archive pass is a separate job.

sources.diff: Initial verbatim voice-specimen archive. Seven existing people records (andre_raw, anne, camila, domenic, eduarda, ren, toni) + six new people records (rodrigo, bibi, rhonna, eufelyn, cathlyn, spyros) all reference this file for their handling.tone / handling.voice_adjustments fields.

rodrigo_souza.title_per_slack: President - CEO  Comeketo & Virtual Kitchen Hall
rodrigo_souza.voice_summary: High-energy captain. Ellipsis-heavy. Emoji-forward. Tags for visibility. Types fast, typos are fine. Uses ALL CAPS for announcements. Reacts more than composes — the compressed reaction (':fire::fire::fire:', ':muscle::skin-tone-5::muscle::skin-tone-5:', ':confetti_ball::fire::eagle:') is more common than full sentences. When he does compose, sentences often end with ' ... ' as a thought-pause. Uses 'Thx' frequently. His skin-tone emoji modifier is always skin-tone-5.
signature_patterns: Trailing ellipsis as thought-pause: 'Food show', 'Missed call …', 'Ok …', 'what the heck ?'
Double-ellipsis or space-ellipsis: '... ...' for a longer pause or transition
Tag for visibility pattern: '<@person> for visibility ...' / '<@person> :eyes:'

Emoji-only reactions to wins: ':confetti_ball::fire::eagle:', ':fire::fire::fire:', ':muscle::skin-tone-5::muscle::skin-tone-5::muscle::skin-tone-5:', ':clap::skin-tone-5::clap::skin-tone-5::clap::skin-tone-5::clap::skin-tone-5:', ':right-facing_fist::skin-tone-5::left-facing_fist::skin-tone-5: :muscle::skin-tone-5: :muscle::skin-tone-5:'
Skin-tone-5 is invariant — every hand/muscle emoji carries this modifier
ALL CAPS for channel-purpose announcements (not for yelling): 'SO BASICALLY IS A PLACE WHERE WE WILL SHARE QUICK COMMUNICACTIONS AND REQUETS ASKS ... FOR OUR AI PROJECTS WITHIN THE COMAPNY'
Asks questions with two question marks and emoji: '?? :weary:' or 'no ??' or '..?'
Fast typer — typos stay: 'brining' (bringing), 'ca you' (can you), 'Shoudnt' (shouldn't), 'COMAPNY' (company), 'COMMUNICACTIONS' (communications), 'REQUETS' (requests)
Brazilian Portuguese accent on English: 'Thx for brining this up to our attention'
Frustration register uses :weary: :tired_face: :triumph: in chains: 'ughhh :weary:', ':tired_face::tired_face::tired_face::tired_face::triumph::triumph::triumph:'
Warmth markers — 'U DA BEST !!!', 'Jake I really like the approach that this guy takes …'
Uses 'guys' as generic plural addressee
Portuguese interjections when appropriate: 'Thx' as abbreviation, 'pls'

bibi_de_los_santos.voice_summary: Professional but warm. Direct. Short sentences. Uses 'hun' as affectionate colleague address. Laughs with 'lol'. Tags @Anne Silva when something crosses into event-execution territory. Routes up to Rodrigo when a decision is above her product layer. Her warmth is not performative — it's quiet and practical. She'll say 'just saw this, sorry. I'll soon' as a genuine apology-for-latency.
signature_patterns: 'hun' as teammate address: 'please be sure to understand what she's looking for hun'
'lol' for self-deprecating: 'confused me here lol'
Short acknowledgements: 'Correct', 'Yes, it's possible', 'yes'
Routes-up register: 'I'm not the right person to help here, Rodrigo probably is <@Toni>'
Latency-apology: 'just saw this, sorry. I'll soon'
Explicit 'don't forget to add to calendar' operational reminders
'TY - L - B' sign-off on longer notes (Love, Bibi)
Tag pattern: 'tagging <@Anne Silva> just in case'
'<!channel> hi team!!' warm channel-wide address with double exclamation
Uses double-exclamation !! as emphasis rather than caps
Operational framing: 'Menu won't be a problem, but timing (after 6:30pm) won't work. Instructed Eduarda to engage with them for a more suitable day/time.'
Offers coverage / backstop: 'feel free to use or reach out to me'

rhonna_ricafort.voice_summary: The structured team-announcement voice. Writes detailed instructional messages with @-tag rosters at the top, then the body, then 'pls confirm understanding'. Warm but firm. Uses 'pls', 'thnx', 'thak you', 'teh' (the typos are fast-typing signature, not carelessness). Heavy use of emoji in playful register: ':rolling_on_the_floor_laughing::party-blob:', ':smiling_face_with_3_hearts:', ':eyes::send-it: BIG EYES HERE :send-it::eyes:'. Caps-lock for urgent alerts. Runs the team cadence — KPI tracking, call-back monitoring, lead-distribution announcements.
signature_patterns: Structured team-announcement format: @-tag roster at top → body → 'pls confirm understanding' or 'pls ck vi comment thread NOT AN EMOTICONS!'
'pls' / 'thnx' / 'thak you' / 'teh' (the) — fast-typing typo signature
ALL CAPS urgency markers: 'TEAM!!!!!!! ALERT!!', 'BIG EYES HERE'
Warm closers: 'thank you <@Eufelyn> you're the best !'
Emoji-sandwich framing: ':eyes::send-it: BIG EYES HERE :send-it::eyes:'
Bulletized directives with • for clear team instructions
'pls make sure we are on top of this thank s' — the closer 'thank s' with space is recurring
Coaching-register when correcting behavior: 'Let's remember that a supportive and understanding environment is built on accountability...not an excuse to be less disciplined in our work.'
Celebration-register for wins: 'Great work team for being on time today and for staying engaged—appreciate it :raised_hands:'
Uses '...' for pause before a redirect: 'Moving forward every calls made Must have an outcome.\nand pls make a little note , thank you'
Sometimes uses 'Hey team' / 'Alright team' as opener
Uses bold via asterisks for line-item highlighting in KPI announcements

andre_raw.voice_summary: Terse, reactive, action-oriented. Most messages are one to four words or a single emoji chain. When he writes at length, it's because he needs help and is in request mode — in which case he writes menu bullets in Portuguese, tags @Rodrigo and @Bibi, and ends with 'thks' or 'please'. His emoji vocabulary is tight: :fire:, :eyes:, :eye::eye:, :rotating_light: (chained when serious). Very mobile-keyboard-fluent — apostrophes often come out as ´ (acute accent), suggesting Brazilian Portuguese keyboard or mobile-set keyboard with that autocorrect. Uses 'btw' and 'kindda'. 'U' for 'you' sometimes. Fluent in both English and Portuguese; switches based on lead's language.
signature_patterns: Single-word or short responses: 'will do', 'Ok', 'on a call now', 'on the phone with', 'I will thanks'
Emoji-only reactions: ':fire:', ':eyes:', ':eye::eye:'
Emoji chains for urgency: ':rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light::rotating_light:'
Acute-accent apostrophe (´ instead of '): 'don´t', 'Toni´s lead', 'I´ll soin' — mobile-keyboard tell
'thks' abbreviation for thanks
Request format when long: menu items as bullet list + tag Rodrigo + tag Bibi + 'please help'
Code-switches to Portuguese for Portuguese-speaking clients: 'Gostaria de pedir orçamento para 50 pessoas...'
Colloquial register: 'you are awesome <@Eduarda>', 'That looks good man'
Concise urgency: 'This is time sensitive please...'
Praise peers directly: 'Smooth operator by <@Spyros> lol'
Self-explains delays: 'My internet failed me when I was trying to jump on the meeting', 'My hub was off for whatever reason that I don´t know'

toni_llagas.voice_summary: Clean, polite, structured. Professional register — 'Good morning guys!' / 'Got it Domenic.' Uses bulleted lists when sharing templates or reference material. Tags the right people (Rodrigo + Bibi + Rhonna) when escalating a client question. Explicitly frames things: 'Question:' / 'Sharing this pricing I got from Rodrigo for future reference:'. Remote worker register — starts day with morning greeting. Can drop occasional edge when frustrated ('I don't get it, Andre, who in their right mind would laugh about a canceled partnership that would benefit our company?'). Filipino-based on profile signals and register.
signature_patterns: Structured question-framing: 'Question:' as explicit header, then the question, then tags
Template-sharing: 'Sharing this pricing I got from Rodrigo for future reference:\n• *Real Fork, Knife & Spoon:* $2.50 per person\n• *Bread & Butter:* $1.99 per person'
Morning greeting: 'Good morning guys!', 'see you at 9:30'
Acknowledgement: 'Got it Domenic.', 'Done', 'Ok'
Tags escalation chain: <@Rodrigo> <@Bibi> <@Rhonna>
When flagging mid-client-request: 'Yes bibi, click open link'
Occasional sharpness when a teammate misbehaves — direct confrontation register
Bulleted lists with bold-via-asterisks formatting

eduarda_fedrizzi.voice_summary: Long, detailed, thorough client-summary voice. Opens with 'Hey' or 'Hi' + tag, then 'I have a question/request for...' or 'I have a client who...'. Breaks asks down by section (Food / Bar / Charcuterie / Bar / Stations / etc.). Uses emoji sparingly but warmly: :pray::skin-tone-3:, :point_down:. Brazilian-Portuguese native — 'hehe' laugh, casual closers. Collaborative — always frames her asks as 'so I can finalize her quote' or 'so I can get back to her'. Portuguese-speaker — handles Portuguese callbacks in the team.
signature_patterns: Openers: 'Hey <@Bibi> & <@Rodrigo>', 'Hi <@Bibi> and <@Rodrigo>!'
Client-summary structure: name + date + venue + guest count, then section-headed asks
Section headers with bold-via-asterisks: '*Charcuterie / Frios Table:*', '*Feijao Tropeiro:*', '*Bar:*'
Warm closers: 'Thanks!', 'Let me know what we can confirm so I can finalize her quote and send it over this week and hopefully book it hehe. Thanks!'
'hehe' Portuguese-pattern laugh in English text
Emoji accents: :pray::skin-tone-3:, :point_down:
Transparency about her thought process: 'I wanted to run this by you both to see if we can do her requests'
Problem-solver mindset: 'I think this could turn into a really nice and unique event for us'
Self-explains: 'I'm here, I ran into a small issue with my HDMI cable and had to look for a backup'
Collaborative: 'Not sure if I should even reply to this or just leave it as is…'
Uses ~$8K shorthand for dollar values

verbatim_specimens.text: mine, trying to get her on a zoom with us
I'm here, I ran into a small issue with my HDMI cable and had to look for a backup so I could get my monitor connected again.
you're welcome! it was quite a long call, she had a lot of questions. I sent her our menus so she can choose what she wants, so she requested to be called back later today once she has a better idea
Not sure if I should even reply to this or just leave it as is…

<@Rodrigo> <@Bibi> Hi guys!
I have a lead who's interested in joining us on the 19th, but they'd only be available after 6:30. Would there be any chance we could still serve them a few options, at least the ones they're most interested in (pork loin, stuffed eggplant, mac & cheese...)?
Please let me know so I can get back to her on that :pray::skin-tone-3:
This is an ~$8K wedding for October 2nd, they're pretty much ready to move forward, they'd just like to try the food first.

Question:

Do we do any of these champagne brands at our champagne service? <@Rodrigo> <@Bibi>

Hi <@Bibi> and <@Rodrigo>! I had a call with Sara today (she came to our last  tasting). I'd like to share summary of a few things I need your input on before I finalize her quote.

*Charcuterie / Frios Table:* She wants a "boteco style" spread: salami, ham, cheese, olives, quail eggs, some fruit (grapes, etc.) and pork rinds (torresmo). Can we do it? What would a custom boteco-style board look like from our end? And what would we charge per person for this setup?

*Feijao Tropeiro:* She mentioned our version felt more Americanized and specifically asked for a traditional Brazilian-style feijao tropeiro. Can we accommodate it? (apparently she would like to taste more cassava flour)

*Bar:* Her crowd drinks mostly beer (Heineken and Corona), caipirinha, and her husband loves whisky. She also needs soft drinks for kids. No wine needed. Since neither beed & wine or full bar fully matches it, can we put together a custom package around this? What would the per person cost look like for this specific setup?

Let me know what we can confirm so I can finalize her quote and send it over this week and hopefully book it hehe. Thanks!

Hey <@Bibi> <@Rodrigo>
I have this client and I wanted to run this by you both to see if we can do her requests
She's planning a graduation party for her daughter, around *40–45 guests*, at her backyard, June 7th
Her idea is more of a *station-style / "barraquinhas" concept*, and she's currently getting quotes from multiple vendors, but mentioned it would be much easier if she could have everything coordinated with one company.
Awesome!!! Thank you sooo much

eufelyn.voice_summary: Support-ops voice. Drops tasting tallies and highlights-in-red enforcement. Short structured updates followed by explicit asks. Uses :clap::clap: for momentum. Mix of polite escalation and deadline urgency: 'Sales notes for the leads highlighted in red are needed today,PLEASE.' — the terminal 'PLEASE' in caps after a comma is distinctive. Tasks the sales team directly without softening. Bold-via-asterisks for update sections.
signature_patterns: Tally-announcement format: 'We have X tastings booked, with a total of Y attendees..:clap::clap:'
Bold-via-asterisks for updates: '*This is our current tasting update:*'
Emoji for arrow-down reference: ':point_down:'
'PLEASE' in caps at sentence-end for emphasis: 'Sales notes for the leads highlighted in red are needed today,PLEASE.'
Don't-forget directive: 'Don't forget to complete your sales notes before EOD TODAY!!'
Direct team-member attention flags
Tracks no-shows and absences operationally: 'Andre is not on the meeting- 2 skip from the lead rotation today.'

cathlyn_lopez.voice_summary: Admin staff — sparse first-person Slack footprint but heavy ClickUp-comment presence. Shows up in the team's @-tag rosters for admin-staff workstreams (tasting coordination, pricing-sheet monitoring, email-template enforcement). Self-title 'Queen of Sleep' is a warm hint at her informal register. Voice-specimen sample is limited — needs a deeper Slack pass to establish her verbatim patterns.
signature_patterns: Primarily appears via ClickUp comments on the #special-requests channel (multiple comment/attachment events in a row), signaling detailed task-comment work
Gets tagged BY Rhonna as part of the 'admin staff' roster: '<@Eufelyn> <@Cathlyn Lopez> <@Camila Cides>'
Task title 'Queen of Sleep' suggests playful self-presentation when not formal

verbatim_specimens.text: (No direct messages in the sampled window — Cathlyn was assigned by Rhonna to 'monitor this process' for tasting-email enforcement, Apr 22)
(ClickUp comment added by Cathlyn Lopez — comment text not in Slack payload; see ClickUp for content)

spyros_marinos.voice_summary: Precise technical voice. Version-stamps Quote Maker releases ('New! In Quote Maker 14.3'). Writes short structured patch notes with backtick-quoted warnings. When responding to a security question, calm and measured: 'theres not much defense to this type of attack, just making sure not to click unknown links in pdfs. Im just happy I was able to catch it...' — no caps, no panic. Dropped 'Ooo nice one' in #ai-topics, suggesting a playful side in tech-talk contexts. Joined in early 2024, rejoined March 2026.
signature_patterns: Version-stamp format: ':timer_clock: *New! In Quote Maker 14.3*' as update header

Backtick-fenced explanation blocks for context: '```This combines with other adjustments we have done lately, so please make sure you are using the link from Resources & SOPS when you are creating a quote, as we are constantly modifying how this works```'

Precise bug-scope statements: 'There was a bug in the old quote maker, anything prior to march 28th. If you have a customer who has additional sides (or mains) that go over the package amounts, please use the new quote maker'
Calm-analytical on incidents (no caps-lock urgency)
'Im' / 'theres' / 'just' — casual contractions in otherwise precise technical prose
Posts new-tool links as single-line references: 'https://getviktor.com/'

anne_silva.voice_summary: Longer, more formal-warm messages when she writes. Structured team-announcement style but with more emotional warmth than Rhonna. Opens with 'Good morning, everyone!' and closes with 'Thank you all!'. Uses structured bullets but speaks in complete sentences. Appreciation-first framing — she acknowledges people doing well BEFORE calling out gaps. Heavy ClickUp comment presence in #special-requests from Jan 2026 — she's deeply embedded in event-detail execution.
signature_patterns: Warm opener: 'Good morning, everyone!'
Appreciation-first framing: 'Some of you have been doing an excellent job by sending them ahead of time, even before my reminder... and I really appreciate that. Thank you!'
Gentle pivot: 'But still... a few invoices are still being missed.'
Instruction with reasoning: 'To avoid delays, please don't leave reminder messages unanswered.'
Warm closers: 'Thank you all!'
Bulleted clarifications without being curt: '• if invoice has been created and it's ready to be sent\n• if something has changed (for example, the quote was updated and payment is not due yet).'
Event-detail register in ClickUp comments (multiple comments in a row on tasks — deep execution engagement)

verbatim_specimens.text:
Good morning, everyone!
I'd like to highlight once again the importance of staying on top of our invoices. This is essential to keeping our operations running smoothly.
Please make sure to send invoices as soon as I send a reminder. Some of you have been doing an excellent job by sending them ahead of time, even before my reminder... and I really appreciate that. Thank you!
But still... a few invoices are still being missed. To avoid delays, please don't leave reminder messages unanswered. When you receive one, let me know right away:
• if invoice has been created and it's ready to be sent
• if something has changed (for example, the quote was updated and payment is not due yet).
Keeping communication clear helps us stay organized and ensures the business keeps moving forward.
Thank you all!

When asking, frames the client quote + structural follow-up: 'Would it be possible to have X prepared half medium and half medium-rare, please?' then quotes the client's ask in a code block. Uses 'please' at start and end of requests. Filipino based on name pattern + Rhonna's roster positioning. Sparse specimens in the sample — needs a deeper pull.

Tags Bibi when asking product questions
'please' at both start and end as politeness frame

domenic_dawson.voice_summary: Historical 2023 VKH-era operations-director voice. Directive imperatives, short sharp lines. 'Cmon guys lets go', '9:30', 'be professional'. Writes meeting summaries with structured markdown (attendees / agenda / action items / key decisions / blockers). Builds org-process — 'lack of templates' as a blocker he names. When giving instructions, uses short labeled commands: '^Clickup Task', 'Note: VKH and Rodrigo'. Thinks in systems. Much less present in recent traffic — may have stepped back operationally, but his voice on the cash-flow sheet ('Please assume an 80% Accuracy... - dom') carries the same register: honest, pragmatic, unpolished.
signature_patterns: Imperative short lines: 'Cmon guys lets go', '9:30', 'be professional'
Structured meeting-minutes format (headers: Agenda / Action Items / Key Decisions / Blockers)
'^' for back-reference annotations: '^Clickup Task'
'Note:' prefix for sidebar annotations
Short chat-stream thinking: 'Camila you're good, this is sales and marketing only' → 'Actually' → 'why not' → 'join up'
Sign-off 'dom' on his own documents
Self-deprecating accuracy caveats: 'Please assume an 80% Accuracy. The old leads were a little messy but i did my best'

rodbot_application_notes: Rodbot should NEVER try to 'be' someone else on Slack — never impersonate Rodrigo or any teammate directly in a channel. Rodbot's job when drafting for a specific person is to produce a DRAFT the person will review and send in their own voice.

When drafting a message that a specific person will send, reach for their signature patterns first, then verbatim specimens second. If no specimen matches the situation, default to their register-tier and let the person edit.

The register-tier for the CHANNEL matters more than the register-tier for the person. Rhonna's structured-rollcall format is for #a_catering_group team-announcements; she writes differently in #special-requests (shorter, more questioning).
Preserve the typos. Rodrigo's 'Thx for brining' and Rhonna's 'thak you' and 'teh' are identity markers, not errors. Rodbot should NOT sanitize them when drafting in their voice.

When composing a celebratory reaction for Rodrigo on a win, pull from his real reaction emoji vocabulary: :fire::fire::fire: / :confetti_ball::fire::eagle: / :muscle::skin-tone-5::muscle::skin-tone-5::muscle::skin-tone-5:. Invented emoji chains will land wrong.

Portuguese code-switching is identity-preserving for Andre, Eduarda, and Rodrigo. If the context warrants Portuguese (a Brazilian client, menu items, warm exclamations), use it as they do — Andre's full-Portuguese client-menu asks, Eduarda's 'hehe', Rodrigo's 'Açaí station ok :white_check_mark:'.
Gaps: Cathlyn, Rena, Camila, and Anne have limited specimens in this first pass. A second pass should pull deeper (search by author, sample multiple months) to fill their profiles out.
